chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for configuring the display representation of the Rheonics sensor error status, which is transmitted to a PLC, within a software interface.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Sensor error status
Modified on: Tue, 12 Oct, 2021 at 1:46 AM

The sensor Error Status is being sent from the Rheonics Sensor to the PLC, this value can be configured to be displayed as binary, hexadecimal or decimal by going to the “Tools” Tab, select “Options”.

Software Interface: RheonicsSRV_eval_4A - Connected Components Workbench Standard Edition

- External Tools...

- Options...

Project Organizer:

  - Micro820
    - Programs
      - Prog1
        - Local Variables
      - Prog2
        - Local Variables
    - Global Variables
    - User-Defined Function Blocks
    - User-Defined Functions
    - DataTypes

Function Block Diagram Elements:
- Block Type: MSG_CIPGENERL, MSG_CIPGENERIC
- Connection Points/Labels: IN, Q, A_CtrlCfg, CtrlCfg, Status, A_AppCfgId, AppCfg, ResLeng_, A_TargetCfg, TargetCfg, A_ReqData, ReqData, A_ReqDataLen, ReqLeng_, ResData, A_Data, A_Status, A_DataLen

Figure 1 Setting different display representation

1. Select the “Project” option and open the Integer box and choose Hexadecimal
key_facts: The sensor error status from a Rheonics Sensor is sent to a PLC.
The display format for the error status (binary, hexadecimal, or decimal) can be configured within the software interface.
To configure the display, users must navigate to the 'Tools' tab and select 'Options'.
One configuration step involves selecting 'Project', opening the 'Integer' box, and choosing 'Hexadecimal' as the display format.
diagram_labels: RheonicsSRV_eval_4A - Connected Components Workbench Standard Edition

chunks.page_summary: This page details how to interpret sensor status bits, which are transmitted as a WORD data type over digital communication channels, indicating various operational states and potential issues.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 2. Display format Hexadecimal

Sensor status is a WORD data type, these status bits can be used over digital communication channels to understand when the sensor is operating correctly and when there is an issue.

The sensor status can take any of the following values:

| Bit      | Hex    | Name                      | Comment                                                                                             |
|----------|--------|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Bit 0    | 0x0001 | PLL frequency mismatch    | The PLL frequency does not match the sensor frequency. Derived from the ASB string (E10)            |
| Bit 1    | 0x0002 | PLL not locked            | The PLL is not locked. Derived from the ASB string (E01)                                            |
| Bit 2    | 0x0004 | PLL lock incorrect        | The PLL has locked on the wrong frequency. Derived from the ASB string (E02)                        |
| Bit 3    | 0x0008 | ASB communication error   |                                                                                                     |
| Bit 4    | 0x0010 | Temperature sensor failed | The temperature sensor has failed. Derived from the ASB string if temperature is -273.0             |
| Bit 5    | 0x0020 | Sensor too hot            | If temperature is above the hardcoded physical temperature limit.                                   |
| Bit 6    | 0x0040 | ASB communication error   |                                                                                                     |
| Bit 7    | 0x0080 | Serial Changed            |                                                                                                     |
| Bit 8    | 0x0100 | Status not clean          | Sensor is not clean.                                                                                |
| Bit 9    | 0x0200 | Status in Air             | Determines if sensor is in air                                                                      |
| Bit 10-15| Unused |                           |                                                                                                     |

Example:
key_facts: Sensor status is a WORD data type.
Sensor status bits can be used over digital communication channels to understand sensor operation and identify issues.
The Options dialog allows setting the numeric display format to Hexadecimal.
Bit 0 (0x0001) indicates PLL frequency mismatch, derived from the ASB string (E10).
Bit 1 (0x0002) indicates the PLL is not locked, derived from the ASB string (E01).
Bit 2 (0x0004) indicates the PLL has locked on the wrong frequency, derived from the ASB string (E02).
Bit 3 (0x0008) indicates an ASB communication error.
Bit 4 (0x0010) indicates the temperature sensor has failed, specifically if the temperature is -273.0.
Bit 5 (0x0020) indicates the sensor is too hot if the temperature is above the hardcoded physical temperature limit.
Bit 6 (0x0040) indicates an ASB communication error.
Bit 7 (0x0080) indicates the serial has changed.
Bit 8 (0x0100) indicates the sensor status is not clean.
Bit 9 (0x0200) determines if the sensor is in air.
Bits 10-15 are unused.
warnings_notes: Bit 5 (0x0020): Sensor too hot - If temperature is above the hardcoded physical temperature limit.
Bit 4 (0x0010): Temperature sensor failed - The temperature sensor has failed. Derived from the ASB string if temperature is -273.0.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays a programming diagram illustrating a sensor status of 16#100, which corresponds to a 'Status not clean/ Sensor is not clean' error, and provides references for troubleshooting.

Notice that the sensor status has a value of 16#100, this corresponds to a Sensor status of “Status not clean/ Sensor is not clean.”, which helps to showcase the following actions a customer can take to fix the issue.

REFERENCE:
* Getting Started (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397129)
* Sensor Error Status and Parameter Status (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393091)
key_facts: A sensor status value of 16#100 indicates that the sensor is not clean.
The document provides links to support articles for getting started and understanding sensor error and parameter statuses.
The programming diagram 'ErrorReadingAssembly' is used to process and display sensor status.
The 'COP 126 COP' block has inputs for Enable, Src, SrcOffset, Dest, DestOffset, Length, and Swap, and outputs for END and Sta.
warnings_notes: Sensor status '16#100' indicates 'Status not clean/ Sensor is not clean.'
